message.content:
Run a second-pass design audit of the CourtOps Tennis app at `C:\Users\vkozy\repos\tennis-app`. A first audit was just completed; its findings + status live in `docs/design-audit.md`. Skim that doc first so you don't re-report what's already known. Focus on:

1. **Regressions introduced by the recent fixes** — read `docs/design-audit.md`, then check whether the diffs landed cleanly. The recent batch touched: `frontend/app.js` (csv export, `api()` rewrite, escape handler, filter matchers, refreshAllSelects rAF, lazy tab loaders, parallel loadAssignments, submit disable, fmtDOW UTC, expandPlayerRef throw, 1-9 tab shortcuts), `frontend/index.html` (cancel labels, DOB required, miles max), `frontend/styles.css` (warn-block tokens, toast theme), `backend/app/routers/auth.py` (rate limit + secure cookie + samesite=strict + session rotation), `backend/app/routers/roster.py` (gender required for new), `backend/app/routers/room_blocks.py` (PUT no longer accepts tournament_id), `backend/app/routers/me.py` (router-level guard), `backend/app/models.py` (DOB required, miles ≤ 1000), `backend/seed.py` (test-player cleanup, 32 demo players).

2. **New findings the first audit missed** — domain bugs, UX dead-ends, broken keyboard nav, accessibility gaps, dead code, security smells beyond CSRF (deferred).

3. **Quality of the fixes themselves** — places where the fix is too narrow, leaves the calling-side broken, or introduces a subtle race / drift.

Hard rules:
- Don't repeat items already marked [x] in `docs/design-audit.md` unless you have new specifics.
- Don't recommend the deferred items again (M11/M12/M13/M19/M22/M24/M26/M27/M28/M29/M34, A47-A52) — those are intentional.
- Be specific: file path + line number + what to change.
- Aim for ~20-40 substantive findings, prioritized Critical / Moderate / Polish. Under 1500 words.

attachment.content:
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- design:user-research: Plan, conduct, and synthesize user research. Trigger with "user research plan", "interview guide", "usability test", "survey design", "research questions", or when the user needs help with any aspect of understanding their users through research.
- design:accessibility-review: Run a WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility audit on a design or page. Trigger with "audit accessibility", "check a11y", "is this accessible?", or when reviewing a design for color contrast, keyboard navigation, touch target size, or screen reader behavior before handoff.
- design:design-handoff: Generate developer handoff specs from a design. Use when a design is ready for engineering and needs a spec sheet covering layout, design tokens, component props, interaction states, responsive breakpoints, edge cases, and animation details.
- design:ux-copy: Write or review UX copy — microcopy, error messages, empty states, CTAs. Trigger with "write copy for", "what should this button say?", "review this error message", or when naming a CTA, wording a confirmation dialog, filling an empty state, or writing onboarding text.
- design:design-system: Audit, document, or extend your design system. Use when checking for naming inconsistencies or hardcoded values across components, writing documentation for a component's variants, states, and accessibility notes, or designing a new pattern that fits the existing system.
- design:design-critique: Get structured design feedback on usability, hierarchy, and consistency. Trigger with "review this design", "critique this mockup", "what do you think of this screen?", or when sharing a Figma link or screenshot for feedback at any stage from exploration to final polish.
- design:research-synthesis: Synthesize user research into themes, insights, and recommendations. Use when you have interview transcripts, survey results, usability test notes, support tickets, or NPS responses that need to be distilled into patterns, user segments, and prioritized next steps.
- data:data-visualization: Create effective data visualizations with Python (matplotlib, seaborn, plotly). Use when building charts, choosing the right chart type for a dataset, creating publication-quality figures, or applying design principles like accessibility and color theory.
- data:write-query: Write optimized SQL for your dialect with best practices. Use when translating a natural-language data need into SQL, building a multi-CTE query with joins and aggregations, optimizing a query against a large partitioned table, or getting dialect-specific syntax for Snowflake, BigQuery, Postgres, etc.
- data:sql-queries: Write correct, performant SQL across all major data warehouse dialects (Snowflake, BigQuery, Databricks, PostgreSQL, etc.). Use when writing queries, optimizing slow SQL, translating between dialects, or building complex analytical queries with CTEs, window functions, or aggregations.
- data:create-viz: Create publication-quality visualizations with Python. Use when turning query results or a DataFrame into a chart, selecting the right chart type for a trend or comparison, generating a plot for a report or presentation, or needing an interactive chart with hover and zoom.
- data:data-context-extractor: Generate or improve a company-specific data analysis skill by extracting tribal knowledge from analysts.
BOOTSTRAP MODE - Triggers: "Create a data context skill", "Set up data analysis for our warehouse", "Help me create a skill for our database", "Generate a data skill for [company]" → Discovers schemas, asks key questions, generates initial skill with reference files
ITERATION MODE - Triggers: "Add context about [domain]", "The skill needs more info about [topic]", "Update the data skill with [metrics/tables/terminology]", "Improve the [domain] reference" → Loads existing skill, asks targeted questions, appends/updates reference files
Use when data analysts want Claude to understand their company's specific data warehouse, terminology, metrics definitions, and common query patterns.
- data:explore-data: Profile and explore a dataset to understand its shape, quality, and patterns. Use when encountering a new table or file, checking null rates and column distributions, spotting data quality issues like duplicates or suspicious values, or deciding which dimensions and metrics to analyze.
- data:build-dashboard: Build an interactive HTML dashboard with charts, filters, and tables. Use when creating an executive overview with KPI cards, turning query results into a shareable self-contained report, building a team monitoring snapshot, or needing multiple charts with filters in one browser-openable file.
- data:statistical-analysis: Apply statistical methods including descriptive stats, trend analysis, outlier detection, and hypothesis testing. Use when analyzing distributions, testing for significance, detecting anomalies, computing correlations, or interpreting statistical results.
- data:analyze: Answer data questions -- from quick lookups to full analyses. Use when looking up a single metric, investigating what's driving a trend or drop, comparing segments over time, or preparing a formal data report for stakeholders.
- data:validate-data: QA an analysis before sharing -- methodology, accuracy, and bias checks. Use when reviewing an analysis before a stakeholder presentation, spot-checking calculations and aggregation logic, verifying a SQL query's results look right, or assessing whether conclusions are actually supported by the data.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Build, debug, and optimize Claude API / Anthropic SDK apps. Apps built with this skill should include prompt caching. Also handles migrating existing Claude API code between Claude model versions (4.5 → 4.6, 4.6 → 4.7, retired-model replacements).
TRIGGER when: code imports `anthropic`/`@anthropic-ai/sdk`; user asks for the Claude API, Anthropic SDK, or Managed Agents; user adds/modifies/tunes a Claude feature (caching, thinking, compaction, tool use, batch, files, citations, memory) or model (Opus/Sonnet/Haiku) in a file; questions about prompt caching / cache hit rate in an Anthropic SDK project.
SKIP: file imports `openai`/other-provider SDK, filename like `*-openai.py`/`*-generic.py`, provider-neutral code, general programming/ML.
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

1 # CourtOps Tennis — Design Audit (2026-05-26) 2 3 Audit run against the working tree; each finding lists file paths and concrete 4 fix suggestions. Items checked off below have been addressed in code. 5 6 ## Critical 7 8 - [x] **C1. Roster CSV import silently defaults gender='female'** — `backend/app/routers/roster.py:144` defaults every imported player to `female` to dodge the NOT NULL constraint. Corrupts gender-aware division filtering for boys imports. 9 *Fix:* parse a `gender`/`sex` column from the CSV; reject rows without it. 10 - [x] **C2. No login rate-limit / lockout** — `backend/app/routers/auth.py:13-32` accepts unlimited POSTs. Add per-IP+username counter with backoff (e.g. 5 fails → 30s sleep). 11 - [x] **C3. Session cookie missing `secure` flag + no token rotation on login** — `backend/app/routers/auth.py:31`. Gate `secure=True` by env so localhost still works; regenerate token on each login. 12 - [x] **C4. Room-block PUT trusts body `tournament_id`** — `frontend/app.js:1491,1506` posts `tournament_id = active.id`; backend currently accepts whatever is sent. Force tournament_id from the URL path on PUT. 13 - [x] **C5. `api()` stringifies FastAPI 422 arrays in toasts** — `frontend/app.js:60-63`. Detail arrays produce `[{"loc":[...]}…]` strings in the UI. Humanize: pick `body.detail[0].msg` when detail is a list. 14 - [x] **C6. `rosterMatches` filters by `JSON.stringify(data)`** — `frontend/app.js:1058-1061,2135-2138`. Typing `123` matches internal `player_id:123`. Restrict to named string fields (name, usta, division, status…). 15 - [x] **C7. `/me` mounted outside admin guard** — `backend/app/main.py:46`. Confirm every endpoint in `me.py` has `get_current_user` dep. Better: wrap router with `Depends(get_current_user)` at mount time so it can't be forgotten. 16 - [x] **C8. Global Escape closes detail modal during cell edit** — `frontend/app.js:646`. Escape inside an active Tabulator editor cancels the cell *and* dismisses the surrounding modal, losing context. Guard with `!document.querySelector('.tabulator-editing')`. 17 - [x] **C9. Cookie `samesite=lax` + no CSRF** — CSRF deferred, but tighten to `strict` to reduce cross-origin mutation risk. 18 - [x] **C10. Hotel confidential popup uses `document.write` with template strings** — `frontend/app.js:2019-2074`. `esc()` doesn't escape ``-style breakouts in meta text. Replace with DOM construction or use the strict `&` escape. 19 20 ## Moderate 21 22 - [ ] **M11. `wireEntity` doing too much** — `frontend/app.js:648-890`. 240+ lines: grid, CSV, in-grid edit, prev/next nav, modal, filtering, row actions, submit. Extract `_buildGrid`, `_buildModal`, `_wireNav`, `_wireSubmit`. 23 - [ ] **M12. Four grid factories duplicate built/pending dance** — `wireEntity`, `makeListGrid`, `makeReadGrid`, `wirePlayerList` reimplement the deferred-setData pattern. Pull into one `buildGrid(mount, options)`. 24 - [ ] **M13. Roster grid duplicates wireEntity master-detail** — `frontend/app.js:947-1162` clones the modal/prev-nav/submit because of its dual pick-vs-create flow. 25 - [x] **M14. `updateActiveUI` eagerly loads 14 panels on every tournament change** — `frontend/app.js:625`. Lazy-load on tab activation (loader map at 540-556 already exists). 26 - [x] **M15. `loadAssignments` does 4 sequential awaits** — `frontend/app.js:1281-1303`. Parallelize sites/blocks/list/avail with `Promise.all`. 27 - [x] **M16. `refreshAllSelects` called by every Setup onLoad** — fires 5× on first paint. 28 - [x] **M17. `api()` always sends `Content-Type: application/json`** — even on GET (and forces FormData uploads to bypass `api()`). 29 - [x] **M18. `api()` `await res.json()` throws on HTML 5xx pages** — surface `status + statusText` on parse error. 30 - [ ] **M19. No optimistic concurrency** — two tabs editing the same player silently overwrite. 31 - [x] **M20. `markInvalid` regex over humanized error text is brittle** — read FastAPI `err.loc` directly. 32 - [x] **M21. `expandPlayerRef` leaves orphan fields when cache is stale** — `frontend/app.js:475-481`. 33 - [ ] **M22. `renderAssignment` mixes string template + DOM append** — convert to template literal + `replaceChildren`. 34 - [x] **M23. `_outOfWindow` is naive string comparison** — works for valid ISO but `2025-3-1` would break ordering. 35 - [ ] **M24. Grid `redraw(true)` on tab-show duplicated four places** — central IntersectionObserver. 36 - [x] **M25. `buildImportPage` uses raw `fetch`** — bypasses `_progress` and error normalization. 37 - [ ] **M26. `enhanceSelect` MutationObserver fires per option add** — `frontend/app.js:410-411`. Disconnect during batch fills. 38 - [ ] **M27. `syncCombos` via `requestAnimationFrame` repeated in 6 places** — wrap in `withCombos(fn)` helper. 39 - [ ] **M28. Shirt sizes hardcoded in 4 files** — `index.html:422-428`, `app.js:993`, `app.js:2083`, `roster.py:79-84`. Source from one place. 40 - [ ] **M29. Cert types hardcoded in 3 files** — same problem. 41 - [x] **M30. No max validation on `one_way_miles`** — pasting `99999` succeeds. Add server cap (e.g. 1000) and HTML `max`. 42 - [x] **M31. Form fields aren't disabled while submit is in flight** — `frontend/app.js:297-304`. User can edit while saving. 43 - [x] **M32. Filter inputs don't debounce** — fine for 32 players, breaks at scale. 44 - [x] **M33. Two competing "Work on →" affordances on Tournament Setup** — `frontend/app.js:2487-2502`. Pick one. 45 - [ ] **M34. `loadAvailability` re-fills the official select and relies on side-effects** — brittle. 46 47 ## Polish 48 49 - [x] **P35. Setup forms ship with `` in HTML** — JS rewrites to "Cancel" only inside wireEntity; other forms still say "New". 50 - [ ] **P36. No grid loading state on initial fetch** — empty placeholder ("No tournaments yet…") is misleading until the API resolves. 51 - [x] **P37. `.needs-active-note` hardcodes `#fff8e6`** — not dark-mode aware. 52 - [x] **P38. `fmtDOW` uses local TZ** — late-night users near UTC boundaries see wrong DOW. 53 - [x] **P39. Toast color always white on accent** — not theme-aware. 54 - [x] **P40. Microcopy mismatch on submit buttons** — "Add player" vs "Update player" vs "Save". Pick one verb. 55 - [ ] **P41. Confirm dialog OK button retains previous label class** when reused. 56 - [ ] **P42. Inconsistent empty states** — Tabulator placeholder vs hand-built `

` (assignments). 57 - [x] **P43. `?` help button is unlabeled visually** — has aria-label but no tooltip / sighted affordance. 58 - [ ] **P44. Focus restoration via DOM node fails when row re-renders** — track by row id. 59 - [x] **P45. `#tshirt-order-table` mixes inline `display:none` with CSS class** — pick one. 60 - [x] **P46. Keyboard shortcut `n` triggers New, but tab-switch is mouse-only** — add `1..9` tab order. 61 62 ## Architectural drift 63 64 - [ ] **A47. `app.js` ~2958 lines, three SPAs in one file** — split via native ESM modules (`setup.js`, `workspace.js`, `me.js`, `grids.js`). 65 - [ ] **A48. `GRIDS` registry + redraw plumbing is a workaround for layout-while-hidden** — `visibility:hidden` panels may obviate. 66 - [ ] **A49. `FILE_TARGETS` + `FORM_MODALS` are parallel registries** of the same set of forms. 67 - [ ] **A50. `models.py` ~594 lines** — split by domain (`models/tournament.py`, `models/roster.py`, …). 68 - [ ] **A51. `roster.py` carries player-upsert + shirt normalization + history JOIN** — extract into shared modules (`playerops.py` already exists). 69 - [ ] **A52. `roster.py` has its own one-off CSV/XLSX import alongside `importer.py`** — consolidate. 70 71 --- 72 73 **Working order:** Critical → Moderate → Polish → Architecture. Items not directly improving correctness/security may be deferred if they require disproportionate refactor; those will be marked as such. 74 75 **Deferred (refactor-heavy, no functional regression):** 76 - M11, M12, M13 — factory consolidation (touches every grid path). 77 - M19 — optimistic concurrency requires schema + UI cascading change. 78 - M22, M24, M26, M27, M34 — small refactors; current code works. 79 - M28/M29 — partial: JS shirt-label source unified; full dedup across HTML + Python needs a generated config. 80 - A47–A52 — architectural splits. 81

1	"""Login / logout / me — cookie-session auth (POC)."""
2	import os
3	import secrets
4	import time
5	from collections import defaultdict
6	from threading import Lock
7	
8	from fastapi import APIRouter, Cookie, Depends, HTTPException, Request, Response
9	
10	from ..db import db_dep
11	from ..models import LoginIn
12	from ..security import COOKIE_NAME, get_current_user, verify_pw
13	
14	router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/auth", tags=["auth"])
15	
16	
17	# In-process rate limiter: keyed on (client_ip, username_lower). 5 failures in
18	# 5 minutes trip a 5-minute lockout. Resets on a successful login. For a POC
19	# this is intentionally in-memory; a multi-worker deployment needs a shared
20	# store (Redis or a `login_attempt` table).
21	_FAIL_WINDOW_S = 300
22	_FAIL_LIMIT = 5
23	_LOCKOUT_S = 300
24	_attempts: dict[tuple[str, str], list[float]] = defaultdict(list)
25	_locked_until: dict[tuple[str, str], float] = {}
26	_lock = Lock()
27	
28	
29	def _record_fail(key: tuple[str, str]) -> None:
30	    now = time.monotonic()
31	    with _lock:
32	        bucket = [t for t in _attempts[key] if now - t < _FAIL_WINDOW_S]
33	        bucket.append(now)
34	        _attempts[key] = bucket
35	        if len(bucket) >= _FAIL_LIMIT:
36	            _locked_until[key] = now + _LOCKOUT_S
37	
38	
39	def _check_lock(key: tuple[str, str]) -> None:
40	    now = time.monotonic()
41	    with _lock:
42	        until = _locked_until.get(key)
43	        if until and now < until:
44	            raise HTTPException(
45	                status_code=429,
46	                detail=f"too many failed attempts; retry in {int(until - now)}s",
47	            )
48	        if until and now >= until:
49	            _locked_until.pop(key, None)
50	            _attempts.pop(key, None)
51	
52	
53	def _clear_attempts(key: tuple[str, str]) -> None:
54	    with _lock:
55	        _attempts.pop(key, None)
56	        _locked_until.pop(key, None)
57	
58	
59	def _secure_cookie() -> bool:
60	    """HTTPS-only cookies in prod; off for localhost dev so browsers accept them."""
61	    return os.getenv("COURTOPS_SECURE_COOKIE", "").lower() in {"1", "true", "yes"}
62	
63	
64	@router.post("/login")
65	def login(body: LoginIn, request: Request, response: Response, conn=Depends(db_dep)):
66	    client_ip = request.client.host if request.client else "?"
67	    key = (client_ip, (body.username or "").strip().lower())
68	    _check_lock(key)
69	    with conn.cursor() as cur:
70	        cur.execute(
71	            "SELECT id, username, password_hash, role, official_id "
72	            "FROM user_account WHERE username = %s",
73	            (body.username,),
74	        )
75	        user = cur.fetchone()
76	        if user is None or not verify_pw(body.password, user["password_hash"]):
77	            _record_fail(key)
78	            raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="invalid username or password")
79	        # Successful auth — rotate any pre-existing token for this user (defends
80	        # against session fixation) and start fresh.
81	        cur.execute("DELETE FROM session WHERE expires_at <= now()")  # opportunistic cleanup
82	        cur.execute("DELETE FROM session WHERE user_id = %s", (user["id"],))
83	        token = secrets.token_urlsafe(32)
84	        cur.execute(
85	            "INSERT INTO session (token, user_id, expires_at) "
86	            "VALUES (%s, %s, now() + interval '30 days')",
87	            (token, user["id"]),
88	        )
89	    _clear_attempts(key)
90	    response.set_cookie(
91	        COOKIE_NAME, token, httponly=True, samesite="strict", path="/",
92	        secure=_secure_cookie(),
93	    )
94	    return {"username": user["username"], "role": user["role"], "official_id": user["official_id"]}
95	
96	
97	@router.post("/logout")
98	def logout(response: Response, sid: str | None = Cookie(default=None), conn=Depends(db_dep)):
99	    if sid:
100	        with conn.cursor() as cur:
101	            cur.execute("DELETE FROM session WHERE token = %s", (sid,))
102	    response.delete_cookie(COOKIE_NAME, path="/")
103	    return {"ok": True}
104	
105	
106	@router.get("/me")
107	def me(user=Depends(get_current_user)):
108	    return {"username": user["username"], "role": user["role"], "official_id": user["official_id"]}

1	"""POC authentication helpers: pbkdf2 password hashing + cookie-session lookup.
2	
3	Not production-grade (localhost POC) but real: salted pbkdf2-sha256 hashes, a
4	server-side session token in an HttpOnly cookie, role-based dependencies.
5	"""
6	import hashlib
7	import hmac
8	import secrets
9	
10	from fastapi import Cookie, Depends, HTTPException
11	
12	from .db import db_dep
13	
14	COOKIE_NAME = "sid"
15	_ITERATIONS = 200_000
16	
17	
18	def hash_pw(password: str) -> str:
19	    salt = secrets.token_hex(16)
20	    dk = hashlib.pbkdf2_hmac("sha256", password.encode(), bytes.fromhex(salt), _ITERATIONS)
21	    return f"pbkdf2_sha256${_ITERATIONS}${salt}${dk.hex()}"
22	
23	
24	def verify_pw(password: str, stored: str) -> bool:
25	    try:
26	        _algo, iters, salt, h = stored.split("$")
27	        dk = hashlib.pbkdf2_hmac("sha256", password.encode(), bytes.fromhex(salt), int(iters))
28	        return hmac.compare_digest(dk.hex(), h)
29	    except Exception:
30	        return False
31	
32	
33	def get_current_user(sid: str | None = Cookie(default=None), conn=Depends(db_dep)) -> dict:
34	    if not sid:
35	        raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="not authenticated")
36	    with conn.cursor() as cur:
37	        cur.execute(
38	            "SELECT u.id, u.username, u.role, u.official_id "
39	            "FROM session s JOIN user_account u ON u.id = s.user_id "
40	            "WHERE s.token = %s AND s.expires_at > now()",
41	            (sid,),
42	        )
43	        user = cur.fetchone()
44	    if user is None:
45	        raise HTTPException(status_code=401, detail="not authenticated")
46	    return user
47	
48	
49	def require_admin(user=Depends(get_current_user)) -> dict:
50	    if user["role"] != "admin":
51	        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail="admin only")
52	    return user
53

4	from ..db import db_dep
5	from ..models import RoomBlockCreate, RoomBlockOut
6	
7	router = APIRouter(prefix="/api/room-blocks", tags=["room-blocks"])
8	
9	_COLS = (
10	    "id, hotel_id, tournament_id, kind, confirmation_number, cancellation_info, "
11	    "check_in, check_out, room_count"
12	)
13	
14	_INSERT = f"""
15	INSERT INTO room_block
16	    (hotel_id, tournament_id, kind, confirmation_number, cancellation_info,
17	     check_in, check_out, room_count)
18	VALUES
19	    (%(hotel_id)s, %(tournament_id)s, %(kind)s, %(confirmation_number)s,
20	     %(cancellation_info)s, %(check_in)s, %(check_out)s, %(room_count)s)
21	RETURNING {_COLS}
22	"""
23	
24	# Note: tournament_id is intentionally NOT updated here — re-assigning a block
25	# between tournaments via PUT would silently move its assignments. If a TD needs
26	# that, they can delete + recreate. See audit C4.
27	_UPDATE = f"""
28	UPDATE room_block SET
29	    hotel_id = %(hotel_id)s, kind = %(kind)s,
30	    confirmation_number = %(confirmation_number)s,
31	    cancellation_info = %(cancellation_info)s, check_in = %(check_in)s,
32	    check_out = %(check_out)s, room_count = %(room_count)s
33	WHERE id = %(id)s
34	RETURNING {_COLS}
35	"""
36	
37	
38	_LIST_COLS = (
39	    _COLS
40	    + ", room_count - (SELECT count(*) FROM assignment a "
41	    "WHERE a.room_block_id = room_block.id) AS rooms_remaining"
42	)
43	
44	
45	@router.get("", response_model=list[RoomBlockOut])
46	def list_room_blocks(tournament_id: int | None = None, kind: str | None = None,
47	                     conn=Depends(db_dep)):
48	    clauses, params = [], []
49	    if tournament_id is not None:
50	        clauses.append("tournament_id = %s"); params.append(tournament_id)
51	    if kind is not None:
52	        clauses.append("kind = %s"); params.append(kind)
53	    where = (" WHERE " + " AND ".join(clauses)) if clauses else ""
54	    with conn.cursor() as cur:
55	        cur.execute(f"SELECT {_LIST_COLS} FROM room_block{where} ORDER BY id", params)
56	        return cur.fetchall()
57	
58	
59	def _with_remaining(cur, block_id: int):
60	    """Re-read a block including the computed rooms_remaining (consistency with list)."""
61	    cur.execute(f"SELECT {_LIST_COLS} FROM room_block WHERE id = %s", (block_id,))
62	    return cur.fetchone()
63	
64	
65	@router.post("", response_model=RoomBlockOut, status_code=201)
66	def create_room_block(body: RoomBlockCreate, conn=Depends(db_dep)):
67	    try:
68	        with conn.cursor() as cur:
69	            cur.execute(_INSERT, body.model_dump())
70	            return _with_remaining(cur, cur.fetchone()["id"])
71	    except psycopg.errors.ForeignKeyViolation as e:
72	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=_fk_detail(e))
73	
74	
75	@router.put("/{block_id}", response_model=RoomBlockOut)
76	def update_room_block(block_id: int, body: RoomBlockCreate, conn=Depends(db_dep)):
77	    try:
78	        with conn.cursor() as cur:
79	            cur.execute(_UPDATE, {**body.model_dump(), "id": block_id})
80	            if cur.fetchone() is None:
81	                raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="room block not found")
82	            return _with_remaining(cur, block_id)
83	    except psycopg.errors.ForeignKeyViolation as e:
84	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail=_fk_detail(e))
85	
86	
87	@router.delete("/{block_id}", status_code=204)
88	def delete_room_block(block_id: int, conn=Depends(db_dep)):
89	    with conn.cursor() as cur:
90	        cur.execute("DELETE FROM room_block WHERE id = %s", (block_id,))
91	        if cur.rowcount == 0:
92	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="room block not found")
93	    return Response(status_code=204)
94	
95	
96	def _fk_detail(e: Exception) -> str:
97	    msg = str(e)
98	    if "hotel" in msg:
99	        return "hotel_id does not exist"
100	    if "tournament" in msg:
101	        return "tournament_id does not exist"
102	    return "invalid reference"

16	# Map many header spellings (USTA exports vary) to canonical roster fields (§3.8).
17	_HEADER_ALIASES = {
18	    "usta_number": {"ustanumber", "usta", "ustano", "ustaid", "usta"},
19	    "first_name": {"firstname", "first", "givenname"},
20	    "last_name": {"lastname", "last", "surname", "familyname"},
21	    "gender": {"gender", "sex", "mf"},
22	    "age_division": {"agedivision", "division", "div", "age"},
23	    "events": {"events", "event"},
24	    "selection_status": {"selectionstatus", "status", "selection"},
25	    "t_shirt_size": {"tshirtsize", "tshirt", "shirt", "shirtsize", "size"},
26	    "dietary_preference": {"dietarypreference", "dietary", "diet", "dietaryrestrictions"},
27	}
28	_VALID_STATUS = {"selected", "alternate", "withdrawn"}
29	
30	
31	def _norm_gender(v):
32	    """Normalize free-text gender to 'male'/'female'; return None on missing/unknown."""
33	    if v is None:
34	        return None
35	    s = str(v).strip().lower()
36	    if s in {"m", "male", "boy", "b", "man"}:
37	        return "male"
38	    if s in {"f", "female", "girl", "g", "woman", "w"}:
39	        return "female"
40	    return None
41	
42	
43	def _norm(h: str) -> str:
44	    return re.sub(r"[^a-z0-9]", "", (h or "").strip().lower())
45	
46	
47	def _canon_headers(headers) -> dict:
48	    """{column_index: canonical_field} for recognised headers."""
49	    alias_to_canon = {}
50	    for canon, aliases in _HEADER_ALIASES.items():
51	        alias_to_canon[canon] = canon
52	        for a in aliases:
53	            alias_to_canon[a] = canon
54	    out = {}
55	    for i, h in enumerate(headers or []):
56	        key = alias_to_canon.get(_norm(str(h) if h is not None else ""))
57	        if key:
58	            out[i] = key
59	    return out
60	
61	
62	def _parse_rows(filename: str, raw: bytes) -> list[dict]:
63	    name = (filename or "").lower()
64	    records = []
65	    if name.endswith((".xlsx", ".xlsm")):
66	        ws = load_workbook(io.BytesIO(raw), data_only=True, read_only=True).active
67	        it = ws.iter_rows(values_only=True)
68	        cmap = _canon_headers(next(it, []) or [])
69	        for r in it:
70	            rec = {key: (r[i] if i < len(r) else None) for i, key in cmap.items()}
71	            if any(v not in (None, "") for v in rec.values()):
72	                records.append(rec)
73	    else:  # CSV / TSV
74	        text = raw.decode("utf-8-sig", errors="replace")
75	        reader = csv.reader(io.StringIO(text))
76	        cmap = _canon_headers(next(reader, []))
77	        for r in reader:
78	            rec = {key: (r[i] if i < len(r) else None) for i, key in cmap.items()}
79	            if any(v not in (None, "") for v in rec.values()):
80	                records.append(rec)
81	    return records

86	        return None
87	    s = str(v).strip()

91	# Canonical t-shirt sizes (must match the roster form dropdown).
92	_SHIRT_CANON = {
93	    ("youth", "small"): "Youth Small", ("youth", "medium"): "Youth Medium",
94	    ("youth", "large"): "Youth Large", ("adult", "small"): "Adult Small",
95	    ("adult", "medium"): "Adult Medium", ("adult", "large"): "Adult Large",
96	    ("adult", "xl"): "Adult Extra Large",
97	}
98	_SHIRT_SIZE = {
99	    "s": "small", "sm": "small", "small": "small",
100	    "m": "medium", "med": "medium", "medium": "medium",
101	    "l": "large", "lg": "large", "large": "large",
102	    "xl": "xl", "xlarge": "xl", "extralarge": "xl", "xxl": "xl", "xxxl": "xl",
103	}
104	
105	
106	def _norm_shirt(v):
107	    """Normalize a free-text t-shirt size (abbreviated or full) to a canonical
108	    label; unrecognized values pass through unchanged so nothing is lost."""
109	    if v is None:
110	        return None
111	    raw = str(v).strip()
112	    s = re.sub(r"[^a-z]", "", raw.lower())  # 'Youth M' -> 'youthm', 'YM' -> 'ym'
113	    if not s:
114	        return raw or None
115	    # Split off a youth/adult marker; default to adult when none is given.
116	    if s.startswith(("youth", "yth", "junior", "jr")) or (s[0] == "y" and len(s) <= 4):
117	        group, rest = "youth", re.sub(r"^(youth|yth|junior|jr|y)", "", s, count=1)
118	    elif s.startswith("adult") or (s[0] == "a" and len(s) <= 4):
119	        group, rest = "adult", re.sub(r"^(adult|a)", "", s, count=1)
120	    else:
121	        group, rest = "adult", s
122	    size = _SHIRT_SIZE.get(rest)
123	    return _SHIRT_CANON.get((group, size), raw)

126	@router.post("/api/tournaments/{tournament_id}/players/import")
127	async def import_roster(tournament_id: int, file: UploadFile = File(...), conn=Depends(db_dep)):
128	    """Upload a CSV/XLSX roster; upsert players (by USTA #) and their entries (§3.8)."""
129	    records = _parse_rows(file.filename, await file.read())
130	    created = updated = upserted = 0
131	    errors: list[str] = []
132	    with conn.cursor() as cur:
133	        cur.execute("SELECT id FROM tournament WHERE id = %s", (tournament_id,))
134	        if cur.fetchone() is None:
135	            raise HTTPException(status_code=404, detail="tournament not found")
136	        for idx, rec in enumerate(records, start=2):  # row 1 = headers
137	            usta = _s(rec.get("usta_number"))
138	            if not usta:
139	                errors.append(f"row {idx}: missing USTA number")
140	                continue
141	            first, last = _s(rec.get("first_name")), _s(rec.get("last_name"))
142	            gender = _norm_gender(rec.get("gender"))
143	            cur.execute("SELECT id FROM player WHERE usta_number = %s", (usta,))
144	            p = cur.fetchone()
145	            if p:
146	                pid = p["id"]
147	                if first or last or gender:
148	                    cur.execute(
149	                        "UPDATE player SET first_name = COALESCE(%s, first_name), "
150	                        "last_name = COALESCE(%s, last_name), "
151	                        "gender = COALESCE(%s, gender) WHERE id = %s",
152	                        (first, last, gender, pid),
153	                    )
154	                updated += 1
155	            else:
156	                # New player: gender is required (NOT NULL + drives the division
157	                # picker). Reject rows without it so the importer doesn't taint
158	                # the catalog with placeholder genders.
159	                if gender is None:
160	                    errors.append(f"row {idx}: missing/unknown gender for new player {usta}")
161	                    continue
162	                cur.execute(
163	                    "INSERT INTO player (usta_number, first_name, last_name, gender) "
164	                    "VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s) RETURNING id",
165	                    (usta, first, last, gender),
166	                )
167	                pid = cur.fetchone()["id"]
168	                created += 1
169	            status = (_s(rec.get("selection_status")) or "selected").lower()
170	            if status not in _VALID_STATUS:
171	                status = "selected"
172	            cur.execute(
173	                """
174	                INSERT INTO tournament_entry
175	                    (tournament_id, player_id, age_division, events, selection_status,
176	                     t_shirt_size, dietary_preference, source)
177	                VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, 'usta_roster')
178	                ON CONFLICT (tournament_id, player_id) DO UPDATE SET
179	                    age_division = EXCLUDED.age_division, events = EXCLUDED.events,
180	                    selection_status = EXCLUDED.selection_status,
181	                    t_shirt_size = EXCLUDED.t_shirt_size,
182	                    dietary_preference = EXCLUDED.dietary_preference
183	                """,
184	                (tournament_id, pid, _s(rec.get("age_division")), _s(rec.get("events")),
185	                 status, _norm_shirt(_s(rec.get("t_shirt_size"))), _s(rec.get("dietary_preference"))),
186	            )
187	            upserted += 1
188	    return {"created_players": created, "updated_players": updated,
189	            "entries": upserted, "errors": errors}

191	# Names are resolved POINT-IN-TIME: the version of the player's name valid as of
192	# the tournament's play_start_date (policy A). Falls back to the current name when
193	# the tournament predates any recorded version. See docs/data-model.md §PlayerHistory.
194	_SELECT = """
195	SELECT e.id, e.tournament_id, e.player_id, e.age_division, e.events,
196	       e.selection_status, e.t_shirt_size, e.dietary_preference,
197	       p.usta_number,
198	       COALESCE(nm.first_name, p.first_name) AS first_name,
199	       COALESCE(nm.last_name,  p.last_name)  AS last_name
200	FROM tournament_entry e
201	JOIN tournament t ON t.id = e.tournament_id
202	JOIN player p ON p.id = e.player_id
203	LEFT JOIN LATERAL (
204	    SELECT first_name, last_name
205	    FROM (
206	        SELECT first_name, last_name, valid_from
207	        FROM player_history WHERE player_id = e.player_id
208	        UNION ALL
209	        SELECT first_name, last_name, updated_at FROM player WHERE id = e.player_id
210	    ) v
211	    WHERE v.valid_from <= t.play_start_date::timestamptz
212	    ORDER BY v.valid_from DESC
213	    LIMIT 1
214	) nm ON true
215	"""
216	
217

220	    with conn.cursor() as cur:
221	        cur.execute(_SELECT + " WHERE e.tournament_id = %s ORDER BY p.last_name, p.first_name",
222	                    (tournament_id,))
223	        return cur.fetchall()

226	@router.post("/api/tournaments/{tournament_id}/players",
227	             response_model=RosterEntryOut, status_code=201)
228	def add_roster_entry(tournament_id: int, body: RosterEntryCreate, conn=Depends(db_dep)):
229	    body.t_shirt_size = _norm_shirt(body.t_shirt_size)  # canonical size (DB CHECK)
230	    try:
231	        with conn.cursor() as cur:
232	            # Inline-create the player if the form sent a USTA # but no player_id —
233	            # lets a TD add a walk-in directly from the Roster form.
234	            pid = body.player_id
235	            if pid is None:
236	                pid = upsert_player(cur, body.usta_number, body.first_name, body.last_name, body.gender)
237	            cur.execute(
238	                """
239	                INSERT INTO tournament_entry
240	                    (tournament_id, player_id, age_division, events,
241	                     selection_status, t_shirt_size, dietary_preference)
242	                VALUES (%s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s, %s)
243	                RETURNING id
244	                """,
245	                (tournament_id, pid, body.age_division, body.events,
246	                 body.selection_status, body.t_shirt_size, body.dietary_preference),
247	            )
248	            new_id = cur.fetchone()["id"]
249	            cur.execute(_SELECT + " WHERE e.id = %s", (new_id,))
250	            return cur.fetchone()
251	    except psycopg.errors.UniqueViolation:
252	        raise HTTPException(status_code=409, detail="player already on this tournament roster")
253	    except psycopg.errors.ForeignKeyViolation:
254	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="tournament_id or player_id does not exist")

content.text: Account upsert via ON CONFLICT(username): if a username already belongs to a *different* official, this PUT silently steals it (changes official_id) and the prior owner can no longer log in. Also the password is sent without any minimum-length validation in `AccountCreate`.

# CourtOps Tennis — Second-Pass Design Audit (2026-05-26)

Diffs from the first batch landed cleanly in most places, but a few fixes are narrower than the audit suggested, and a handful of new issues were missed. ~30 substantive findings below.

## Critical

**N1. `upsert_player` still hard-codes `gender='female'`** — `backend/app/playerops.py:24`. C1 was fixed in `roster.py` only; the shared helper used by **every Part B filing path** (late entries, withdrawals, doubles, pairing, hotels, scheduling, divflex) still injects `'female'` for any new player without a gender. Forwarded emails for boys quietly create female records. *Fix:* propagate the same "missing gender → reject" behavior, or look up the player's gender from existing records before defaulting; if a default truly is unavoidable, mark the row `gender_unknown` and surface a TD task to fix.

**N2. `seed.py` cleanup deletes legitimate players with `birthdate IS NULL`** — `backend/seed.py:147`. The PlayerOut model explicitly tolerates NULL birthdates because roster-inline-create and inbox upserts insert without one (`upsert_player` doesn't write a birthdate). Re-running the seed after real use **wipes every player added via Roster "+ New player not on file" or any Part B file flow**, cascading through 8 child tables. *Fix:* drop the `birthdate IS NULL` clause; rely on the name/USTA-shape filters only. Same risk for `first_name IS NULL OR last_name IS NULL` since `upsert_player` accepts both as optional.

**N3. `Player` PUT requires birthdate, blocking edits of pre-existing players** — `backend/app/models.py:104` + `backend/app/routers/players.py:62`. Players created via roster/inbox have NULL birthdate (allowed by PlayerOut); opening one in the Setup → Players form and clicking Save fails validation because the form's `birthdate` is empty. The fix that made DOB required on POST should have left PUT permissive (or auto-filled from an existing row). *Fix:* make `birthdate` optional on `PlayerCreate`, keep the HTML `required` only on the New-Player form path, or use a separate `PlayerUpdate` model.

**N4. `set_official_account` lets an admin steal any username** — `backend/app/routers/officials.py:95`. `ON CONFLICT (username) DO UPDATE` overwrites `official_id` and `password_hash` even when the conflicting row belongs to someone else (including `admin`). Posting `{username:"admin", password:"x"}` to `/api/officials/{id}/account` reassigns the admin account to that official's `official_id`, *changing its password*, and the rotation on line 107 deletes admin's sessions. *Fix:* select first; if the username belongs to another `user_account` row, raise 409.

**N5. `update_my_profile` silently drops `lat`/`lng`** — `backend/app/routers/me.py:39-46`. The SQL omits both columns even though `OfficialCreate` accepts them and the admin official PUT writes them. An official who edits anything via the self-service form zeros out their geocoded location on the *next* admin edit (lat/lng come back from the model but are never persisted), and round-tripped geocoded distances drift. *Fix:* add `lat=%(lat)s, lng=%(lng)s` to the `me.py` UPDATE.

**N6. Login rate-limit dict grows unbounded** — `backend/app/routers/auth.py:24-25`. `_attempts` is keyed on `(client_ip, username_lower)` and `_record_fail` runs on every 401 *including* unknown usernames. A password sprayer hitting `/api/auth/login` with random usernames adds an entry per attempt; nothing prunes it. Memory grows for the process lifetime. *Fix:* (a) cap the dict at N entries (LRU) or (b) periodically GC entries with bucket length < `_FAIL_LIMIT` and `now - last > _FAIL_WINDOW_S`. Also clamp `body.password` length on `LoginIn` (currently unbounded → 10MB pbkdf2 DoS).

**N7. `_humanizeDetail` returns "request failed" when `body.detail` is an *object without msg*** — `frontend/app.js:54-65`. A 400 with `{detail:{code:"x"}}` (no `msg`) collapses to the generic statusText. Minor compared to C5 but worth: fall back to `JSON.stringify(detail)` when the shape is unfamiliar.

## Moderate

**N8. `onSubmit` re-enables form fields with stale `disabled` state** — `frontend/app.js:332-342`. `wasDisabled` is captured before disabling; after submit, every element is restored to its original `disabled` state — but if the submit handler itself toggled controls (e.g. `rosterSetMode` flipping pick/new fields, or the doubles `wants_random` checkbox hiding partner select), the user's mode change is reverted. *Fix:* only re-enable inputs that were enabled at start AND remain in the active row/mode, or snapshot via a class.

**N9. `onSubmit` also disables Cancel during submit** — same block. The user can't escape a stuck request. *Fix:* exclude `[type=button].cancel, [type=button].new-btn` from the disable loop, or only disable elements with `[name]`.

**N10. `expandPlayerRef` throw breaks file-from-email flow without recovery** — `frontend/app.js:533`. M21 raised this from silent submit to user-facing error, but the error caught by the caller calls `setMsg(..., err.message, false)` then **leaves the modal open with the now-stale form**. If `playersById` is stale, no amount of re-clicking helps because the modal doesn't trigger a player refresh. *Fix:* on the error, call `playersCrud.refresh()` then `fillPlayerRefs()` before re-throwing.

**N11. Race in `_tournamentLoaders` initialization** — `frontend/app.js:599-616`. The map is filled lazily inside the tab click handler. If `updateActiveUI` runs before the user ever clicks a tab (e.g. on initial load when `setActive` restores a saved tournament), `_tournamentLoaders` is `{}` and `if (active && _tournamentLoaders[activePanel])` silently no-ops — the active panel's data doesn't load until the user clicks a tab. *Fix:* hoist the map to module scope (it's a static literal).

**N12. `rosterSubmit` button is disabled inside `wrapped` form submit, but the `cancel` button label is unset for the roster form** — `frontend/app.js:1252`. Sets `.cancel` text to "Cancel" at module load; the cancel button visible by default in HTML still says "Cancel"/"New" depending on the source HTML — that's fine — but the roster's `.delete` button is referenced by `wireEntity` and doesn't exist for the roster form. Not regressive but flag: P35 was claimed complete only for `wireEntity` forms; the roster form's cancel/new dance is bespoke.

**N13. `_tournamentLoaders` includes `panel-t-tshirt-order` and `panel-t-availability`, but those tabs also need `refreshAllSelects` first** — `frontend/app.js:604-605`. `loadAvailability` calls `fillSelect(... avail-official, Object.values(officialsById)...)`. On a tab click before `adminInit` finishes, `officialsById` is `{}` and the official picker is empty until the user re-clicks. *Fix:* await `adminLoaded`/`officialsCrud.refresh()` before opening tournament tabs, or refetch officials in `loadAvailability`.

**N14. `loadAssignments` runs four parallel awaits — but each call still bypasses the in-flight progress aggregation** — `frontend/app.js:1384`. Each `api()` increments `_inflight` separately; that's fine. But errors in any of the four reject `Promise.all` and **none of the other three results are used** — partial data is lost. For tournament-scoped data, a sites fetch failure shouldn't blank the official picker. *Fix:* `Promise.allSettled` + per-result error toast.

**N15. `withdrawal` per-row "Withdraw…" jumps tabs *before* setting `player_ref`** — `frontend/app.js:1096-1100`. The tab click fires `loadWithdrawals` synchronously which can clear the form; then `wdForm.player_ref.value = e.player_id` runs in the next microtask; but the tab click handler's `_tournamentLoaders[...]` loader is `async`, so a race exists where the form opens with player_ref set to "" because the load arrived later. *Fix:* await the loader before setting the value, or set the value *and* call `requestAnimationFrame(syncCombos)` in the loader's `then`.

**N16. `closeOpenDetail` runs on Esc *globally* — but the shortcuts modal Escape handler runs first via the keydown listener** — `frontend/app.js:206`. When the shortcuts modal is open, pressing Esc fires both listeners: the first hides it; the second sees no `.tabulator-editing` and calls `closeOpenDetail()`. If another modal is open behind shortcuts (unusual but possible from a quick keyboard flow), it closes too. *Fix:* `return` from the global Esc handler if the shortcuts modal was visible at the start of the event tick.

**N17. `confirmDialog` Enter binding clashes inside text inputs** — `frontend/app.js:161`. The modal listens globally on `keydown`; if the OK button's focus is lost (any click elsewhere in the modal) Enter still resolves true. Low risk, but the modal also doesn't trap focus, so Tab lets the user escape into the underlying form. *Fix:* trap focus inside `.modal-box` or set `inert` on the rest of the document.

**N18. `_renderTshirtOrder` injects size into `data-size` via `esc()` but trusts it on read** — `frontend/app.js:2282,2311`. `i.dataset.size` is later sent to the API. If the backend ever returns an unknown `size` code, the PUT body forwards it; if the backend stores it permissively the inventory ends up keyed on garbage. *Fix:* validate against `_SHIRT_CODES` before assembling `on_hand`.

**N19. Hotel confidential popup script-injection of `window.print()`** — `frontend/app.js:2179`. C10 fix says esc() blocks `</style>` breakouts, but the popup also contains `<script>window.addEventListener("load", () => setTimeout(() => window.print(), 250));</script>` — fine here, but if `window.open` is blocked the function shows a toast and returns; on subsequent attempts the previously-opened popup (if user dismissed the block) is *orphaned*. Minor. The bigger issue: the inline `onclick="window.print()"` and `window.close()` attributes rely on the new document's CSP being permissive — if CSP gets tightened later this silently breaks. *Fix:* attach listeners after `document.close()` instead of using inline handlers.

**N20. `fmtDOW` is UTC-correct but `_datesInRange` parses with local TZ** — `frontend/app.js:1621`. `new Date(start + "T00:00:00")` uses local midnight; for a TD west of UTC, looping `setDate(+1)` across a DST spring-forward boundary skips a day's slice() output (the `+1` day actually advances 23h and the next ISO slice can equal the previous). Real-world: rare in US tournament windows but breaks for March/November ranges. *Fix:* parse as `"T00:00:00Z"` and use `getUTCDate()`/`setUTCDate()`.

**N21. `tShirtOrderState` reads `data.ordered_at` but the cancel handler doesn't update `_tshirtOrderState`** — `frontend/app.js:2330`. After `cancelTshirtOrder`, `loadTshirtOrder()` repopulates the table, but `_tshirtOrderState` is the *old* object until the next call returns. A double-click "Cancel" then "Place" within ~1 RAF reads the stale `ordered_at` and confirms with the wrong message. *Fix:* set `_tshirtOrderState = null` synchronously after the DELETE succeeds.

**N22. `LoginIn` has no length cap** — `backend/app/models.py:567`. Combined with N6, a single attacker can send `password: "A" * 10_000_000` and pin a CPU on pbkdf2 (200k iters). *Fix:* `username: str = Field(max_length=64)`, `password: str = Field(max_length=256)`.

**N23. `cancelTshirtOrder` doesn't pass the snapshot date in the confirm message** — `frontend/app.js:2329`. Cosmetic copy improvement; users won't recall which order they're cancelling.

## Polish

**N24. P40 "one verb" only partially applied** — submit buttons: `index.html:432` still has hard-coded "Add player"; `index.html:450` "Add official"; `index.html:477` "Add block". The JS rewrites them to "Save"/"Update X" in the edit path, but the initial HTML render shows three different verbs across the workspace. *Fix:* settle on "Save" in HTML; let the JS edit-mode rename to "Save" (no change) so users see one verb.

**N25. The shortcuts modal lists `?` but the help button has no visible hotkey** — `frontend/index.html:16`. P43 marked complete (tooltip exists), but sighted-keyboard users can't discover "?" without hovering. *Fix:* add `<kbd>?</kbd>` next to the button text.

**N26. `markRequiredFields` only walks `form .row label`** — `frontend/app.js:3046`. The Tournament workspace Late/Withdrawal/Doubles forms use `.row` consistently, but the player-hotel form's pairing-member rows (`.pmember`) and the t-shirt order inline inputs have no row wrapper — required asterisks don't render. Low impact; verify and either add the class or broaden the selector.

**N27. CSS dark-mode tokens for `.toast` set `--toast-ink: #fff`** — `styles.css:26`. The toast's accent backgrounds (`var(--ok)` / `var(--bad)`) are *already* light/dark-aware; forcing white ink keeps the look right but the explicit override duplicates the default. Worth confirming `--accent-ink` isn't a better source.

**N28. `+ New player not on file →` toggle text doesn't sync `aria-pressed`** — `frontend/app.js:1187-1200`. Screen readers don't know the toggle is stateful. *Fix:* `toggle.setAttribute("aria-pressed", mode === "new" ? "true" : "false")`.

**N29. `1-9` tab shortcut focuses the tab button but doesn't move screen-reader attention to the panel** — `frontend/app.js:226`. After `tabs[idx].click()`, focus stays on the tab. ARIA-tab pattern expects optional roving tabindex. Low priority but a fixed-effort improvement: `panel.focus({preventScroll:true})` or `setAttribute('tabindex', '-1')` on the panel and focus that.

**N30. `_humanizeDetail` truncates to 3 errors silently** — `frontend/app.js:57`. If a CSV import produces 50 errors the user only sees 3 with no "…and 47 more" hint. *Fix:* append `+ N more` when `detail.length > 3`.

**N31. Stale `e.preventDefault()` calls inside handlers wrapped by `onSubmit`** — `frontend/app.js:1545, 1605, 1793, 1927, 1964, 2031, 2378, 2450`. `onSubmit` already calls `e.preventDefault()` at line 327, so these are redundant. Harmless but distracting; pick one place.

**N32. Roster `+ New` button label inconsistency** — `frontend/index.html:385` reads `+ New`, but the audit doc P35 marked HTML cancel labels fixed; the new-button label for Roster is still "+ New" (matching wireEntity convention). Confirm intent.

**N33. `_dowLong` and `_fmtMDY` exist in `frontend/app.js:2536-2539` only for reports** — these are essentially the same TZ-safe parsing pattern as `fmtDOW`. Pull into one helper to avoid drift if the timezone strategy changes again.

---

**Bottom line:** the highest-impact items are N1 (gender corruption regression), N2 (seed eats real players), N3 (cannot edit historical players), N4 (admin-account theft via PUT), N5 (lat/lng data loss), and N6/N22 (auth DoS surface). These together would re-open or partially re-open audit items C1, C7, and add a new privilege-escalation path the first audit missed.
